mission.title: woodpecker-ci: Slow authorization if user has many orgs/repos
mission.description: Slow authorization if user has many orgs/repos. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify woodpecker-ci troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your woodpecker-ci deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review woodpecker-ci configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant woodpecker-ci configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get configmap -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=woodpecker-ci
```
### Component

server

### Describe the bug

Slow authorization if user has many orgs/repos.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Slow authorization if user has many orgs/repos
steps.description:
This PR is a solution for #2502.

To resolve the issue a configuration option `WOODPECKER_ASYNC_REPOSITORY_UPDATE` has been added to allow administrators to decide how Woodpecker should deal with the repository update call that is part of the login flow. It can be either sync/async.
Sync is do-able for smaller organisations with few repositories, but larger organisations might opt for async to improve login speed at the cost of consistency. The repository list will be eventually consistent

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Slow authorization if user has many orgs/repos is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci -n woodpecker-ci --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n woodpecker-ci --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This PR is a solution for #2502.

To resolve the issue a configuration option `WOODPECKER_ASYNC_REPOSITORY_UPDATE` has been added to allow administrators to decide how Woodpecker should deal with the repository update call that is part of the login flow. It can be either sync/async.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with woodpecker-ci installed or the issue environment reproducible.
